content.full_text:
August 13, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: Request for a Formal Interpretation

As the City of Eureka is currently in the process of reviewing its personnel policies and procedures, it has come to our attention that an inconsistency exists in the administration of industrial disability retirements. Under Section 21025.2 of the Government Code, employers are required to compensate employees for their accumulated sick leave upon a disability retirement. It has been past practice of the City not to compensate employees for their accumulated sick leave as provided under the government code.

An employee that has retired on an industrial disability without the benefit of compensation of accumulated sick leave was recently elected to the City Council and is due to take office on November 20, 1990. Upon review of the applicable code sections, there appears to be no provision that would prevent the City from compensating this individual for the accumulated sick leave. Because of the potential conflict, the City of Eureka does request a formal interpretation by the Fair Political Practices Commission on this matter.

The City would appreciate your review of this matter, and letting us know your interpretation. If you require additional information, please contact David W. Tyson, Director of Finance, at (707) 443-7331 ext. 253.

Sincerely,

David R. Tooley
City Manager
City of Eureka

DT/DWT/kg

ADMSEC/DTMisc
